entry.title: Bringing research to life through human stories
entry.summary: It’s the end of an era for our MRC Insight blog which is closing, along with other UKRI blogs. But science storytelling is here to stay. Find out how.

entry.title: AHRC forward look: summer 2026
entry.summary: This blog outlines the broad range of funding opportunities AHRC will publish over the coming months, and the wider portfolio they are part of.

entry.title: Men Minds: young men making time for young men
entry.summary: Providing space, reducing stigma, and normalising conversations among young men to better support their mental health.

entry.title: From ward rounds to speech sounds: an MRC Clinical Fellow’s story
entry.summary: Read advice from an MRC Clinical Research Training Fellow on crafting your application, plus the value of working with patients and sharing your science.

entry.title: Improving MRC funding through continued community engagement
entry.summary: Following our Academy of Medical Sciences open online meeting, we’re sharing community feedback and providing more detail on our new funding process.

entry.title: The lesson that changed how I share science forever
entry.summary: How receiving messages from patients and their families has changed the course of a dementia scientist’s PhD research.

entry.title: MRC funding update
entry.summary: An update on MRC funding during UKRI’s transition, what’s changing, and the next steps for paused opportunities and reopening timelines.

entry.title: Unlocking spin-out intelligence: a dashboard for UK innovation
entry.summary: Read about our road to unlocking insights on university spinning out – developing a strategic spin-out dashboard to better understand UK innovation and growth.

entry.title: The UK facility powering a global neutrino experiment
entry.summary: If you were to ask anyone on the streets of Daresbury village where the DUNE factory was, you’d be met with puzzled looks.

entry.title: Championing EDI in bioscience
entry.summary: Reflections on advancing BBSRC’s work through its Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Expert Advisory Group.
